summary: Dependency-confusion bait installs SSH backdoor and exfiltrates secrets on CI runners
details: The package exports a single function `command()` whose body performs three offensive actions targeting CI/build hosts: (1) Persistence — `index.js:9` shells out to `mkdir -p /home/gitlab-runner/.ssh && echo '<hardcoded-pubkey>' >> /home/gitlab-runner/.ssh/authorized_keys && chmod 600...`, appending an attacker-controlled SSH public key to the gitlab-runner account's authorized_keys to grant persistent remote SSH access. (2) Credential / flag theft — `index.js:14-19` reads `/root/root.txt`, `/home/internal/user.txt`, `/home/gitlab-runner/user.txt`, and `.git/config` files (which typically embed HTTPS basic-auth credentials) and prints their contents to stdout, where CI job logs capture them for whoever triggered the build. (3) Reconnaissance — `index.js:23` runs `ls -la /home/; cat /etc/passwd | grep sh$` and logs results to enumerate user accounts with login shells. The package has no README, no description, no author, no repository, and no real library functionality — the only export is the offensive payload. The bare `internallib_v557` name is shaped as dependency-confusion bait: a build pipeline that mis-resolves an internal-namespace dependency to the public registry would pull this package and execute the payload the first time `command()` is invoked.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
